title: Monotone Lagrangians in cotangent bundles
speaker: Luis Diogo
abstract: We show that there is a 1-parameter family of monotone Lagrangian tori in the cotangent bundle of the 3-sphere with the following property: every compact orientable monotone Lagrangian with non-trivial Floer cohomology is not Hamiltonian-displaceable from either the zero-section or one of the tori in the family. The proof involves studying a version of the wrapped Fukaya category of the cotangent bundle which includes monotone Lagrangians. Time permitting, we may also discuss an extension to other cotangent bundles. This is joint work with Mohammed Abouzaid.

proof_stages.label: From exact to monotone Lagrangians
proof_stages.summary: The talk motivates replacing the difficult exact nearby-Lagrangian problem in cotangent bundles by a Floer-theoretic study of closed monotone Lagrangians in T^*S^3, defining monotonicity via the proportionality of symplectic area and Maslov index.

proof_stages.label: Monotone comparison families in T^*S^3
proof_stages.summary: Lefschetz and Morse-Bott Lefschetz descriptions of T^*S^3 are used to build one-parameter monotone families from loops in the base, with the load-bearing family for the theorem being the monotone Lagrangian tori T^3_tau.

proof_stages.label: Floer-detection theorem and geometric meaning
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem says that any closed orientable spin monotone Lagrangian with defined nonzero Floer theory has nonzero Floer homology either with the zero section S^3 or with some monotone torus T^3_tau, hence is nondisplaceable from one of these comparison objects.

proof_stages.label: Monotone wrapped Fukaya category
proof_stages.summary: The proof is organized inside a monotone wrapped Fukaya category whose objects include the closed monotone Lagrangians and a cotangent fiber, whose morphisms are Floer cochain complexes with A-infinity polygon operations over a Novikov field with valuation.

proof_stages.label: Cotangent-fiber generation and Yoneda modules
proof_stages.summary: Using Abouzaid-type generation, the cotangent fiber generates the wrapped category, so the Yoneda functor L -> CW^*(F,L) into modules over A=CW^*(F,F) is cohomologically fully faithful and converts the geometric problem into a module-theoretic one.

proof_stages.label: Proper module classification by S^3 and T^3_tau
proof_stages.summary: For T^*S^3 the relevant A-infinity module category is treated as formally equivalent to ordinary cohomological modules, and its proper subcategory is split-generated by the Yoneda images of S^3 with degree-three bounding cochains and of the monotone tori T^3_tau with unitary local systems.

proof_stages.label: Unwinding generation to Floer nonvanishing
proof_stages.summary: Split generation of proper modules pulls back through the fully faithful Yoneda functor to generation of the compact Fukaya category, forcing any nonzero object to have nonzero Floer homology with at least one of the listed generators and thereby proving the stated detection theorem.

examples.name: Monotone S^1 x S^2 from a Lefschetz fibration
examples.note: Loops around the two critical values in the affine-quadric Lefschetz fibration lift to monotone Lagrangians topologically S^1 x S^2, illustrating how monotone Lagrangians in T^*S^3 arise although the proof later uses the torus family instead.
examples.engagement_reason: the speaker explicitly runs the Lefschetz-fibration construction: loops in the base lift via vanishing S^2 cycles to an S^1×S^2 Lagrangian, with monotonicity constant τ=A/2 and a one-parameter family by varying the enclosed area.

examples.name: Higher-dimensional cotangent-bundle analogues
examples.note: In response to a question, the speaker notes that analogous constructions in T^*S^5 give Lagrangians such as S^1 x S^2 x S^2 and in general families like S^1 x S^{n-1}, serving only to situate the T^*S^3 construction.
examples.engagement_reason: higher-dimensional cotangent-bundle analogues are only described in passing, with topologies such as T*S^5 giving S^1×S^2×S^2 or generally S^1×S^{n-1}, but no concrete construction or calculation is carried out.

examples.name: SYZ mirror superpotential with affine-line critical locus
examples.note: The Chan-Pomerleano-Ueda mirror-symmetry calculation produces a superpotential whose critical locus is a complex affine line, illustrating why one expects a one-parameter family of nontrivial Lagrangians on the symplectic side.
examples.engagement_reason: the SYZ mirror superpotential is cited from Chan–Pomerleano–Ueda and its critical locus is stated to be an affine line, but the superpotential calculation is not performed in the talk.

examples.name: Flux-type deformation of two zero sections
examples.note: The speaker compares the torus family to Seidel-style abstract flux, suggesting that an H^3-class deforms two copies of the zero section into the one-parameter torus family, as geometric intuition rather than a proof step.
examples.engagement_reason: the flux-type deformation of two zero sections is presented as a vague interpretive picture related to Seidel’s abstract flux, without actually carrying out a deformation or checking it concretely.
